perturbation.original_text: Restricted Period ” shall mean the period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending on the date which is four (4) years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Restricted Period ” shall mean the period commencing on the date of this Agreement and ending on the date which is two (2) years from the date of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the 'Restricted Period' from four years to two years. This will create confusion when other sections reference the 'Restricted Period,' leading to disputes regarding how long the non-compete obligations are in effect, making enforcement uncertain.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller hereby agrees that, during the Restricted Period, except as permitted by Section 5 of this Agreement, it will not, directly or indirectly, own, manage, operate, control, invest in or acquire an interest in, or otherwise engage or participate in the establishment, management or operation of, any Competitive Business that sells or distributes Competitive Products, directly or indirectly, for ultimate purchase by consumers in the Protected Market, without regard to whether the Competitive Business has any office, manufacturing or other business facilities within the Protected Market.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the 'Restricted Period' from four years to two years. The contradicted text states that the seller is restricted from certain competitive actions 'during the Restricted Period'. Because the duration of the 'Restricted Period' is changed from four years to two years, it directly impacts the duration of the seller's obligations as stated in the contradicted text, creating a direct conflict and uncertainty, as per the provided explanation and the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Protected Market ” shall mean the United States.
perturbation.changed_text: Protected Market ” shall mean the State of Delaware.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by narrowing the scope of the 'Protected Market' from the United States to the State of Delaware. This contradicts any other reference to the 'Protected Market' since now it's uncertain what territory the non-compete applies to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller hereby agrees that, during the Restricted Period, except as permitted by Section 5 of this Agreement, it will not, directly or indirectly, own, manage, operate, control, invest in or acquire an interest in, or otherwise engage or participate in the establishment, management or operation of, any Competitive Business that sells or distributes Competitive Products, directly or indirectly, for ultimate purchase by consumers in the Protected Market, without regard to whether the Competitive Business has any office, manufacturing or other business facilities within the Protected Market.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Protected Market' as the United States, while the changed text narrows it to the State of Delaware. The contradicted text refers to activities related to 'Competitive Products, directly or indirectly, for ultimate purchase by consumers in the Protected Market'. This creates a clear contradiction because the scope of the restricted activities is now ambiguous. The original scope included the entire United States, but the modified text restricts it to Delaware. This creates an in-text contradiction of the 'Inconsistencies' type, where different sections of the contract (definition vs. obligation) provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information ” shall mean all customer and supplier lists, marketing arrangements, business plans, projections, financial information, training manuals, pricing manuals, product development plans, market strategies, internal performance statistics and other competitively sensitive information of Seller used solely in the Business and not generally known by the public, whether or not in written or tangible form.
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information ” shall mean only customer lists and financial information of Seller used solely in the Business and not generally known by the public, whether or not in written or tangible form.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction by limiting the scope of 'Confidential Information' to only customer lists and financial information. This conflicts with other sections that rely on the broader original definition, making it unclear which information is protected. The change narrows definition contradicts the obligations related to non-disclosure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller hereby agrees that, during the Restricted Period, it will not, directly or indirectly, disclose to anyone, or use or otherwise exploit for its own benefit or for the benefit of anyone other than Purchaser, any Confidential Information, except as permitted by Section 5 of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Seller may make disclosure of Confidential Information if Seller reasonably concludes that public disclosure of Confidential Information is required by applicable legal requirements and Seller (i) gives Purchaser written notice of such proposed disclosure as far in advance of such disclosure as is reasonably practicable, (ii) cooperates reasonably with Purchaser in its efforts to protect the information from disclosure, including, without limitation, assisting Purchaser in obtaining, at Purchaser’s expense, an appropriate protective order or other reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such information, and (iii) limits its disclosure to the minimum required by applicable legal requirements unless Purchaser agrees in writing to a greater level of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only 'customer lists and financial information', whereas the original definition included a much broader set of items such as 'supplier lists, marketing arrangements, business plans, projections, financial information, training manuals, pricing manuals, product development plans, market strategies, internal performance statistics and other competitively sensitive information'. The contradicted text refers to 'any Confidential Information' and restricts the Seller from disclosing or using it. By narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information', the changed text now only protects a smaller subset of information, creating a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which assumes a broader scope of protection. The seller would be contractually obligated to protect the originally defined 'Confidential Information' but, under the revised text, would only be obligated to protect the limited definition.

perturbation.original_text: 8. Injunctive Relief . Seller acknowledges that (i) any violation of this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to Purchaser, (ii) damages at law would not be reasonable or adequate compensation to Purchaser for violation of this Agreement, and (iii) Purchaser shall be entitled to have the provisions of this Agreement specifically enforced by preliminary and permanent injunctive relief without the necessity of proving actual damages and without posting bond or other security as well as to an equitable accounting of all earnings, profits and other benefits arising out of any such violation.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. Injunctive Relief . Seller acknowledges that (i) any violation of this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to Purchaser, (ii) damages at law would not be reasonable or adequate compensation to Purchaser for violation of this Agreement, and (iii) Purchaser shall be entitled to have the provisions of this Agreement specifically enforced, but only after proving actual damages and posting a bond or other security as well as to an equitable accounting of all earnings, profits and other benefits arising out of any such violation.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the provision for injunctive relief by now requiring proof of actual damages and the posting of a bond. This makes it more difficult for the Purchaser to obtain injunctive relief, contrasting with the original intent of immediate enforcement without those requirements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Seller acknowledges that (i) any violation of this Agreement will result in irreparable injury to Purchaser, (ii) damages at law would not be reasonable or adequate compensation to Purchaser for violation of this Agreement, and (iii) Purchaser shall be entitled to have the provisions of this Agreement specifically enforced, but only after proving actual damages and posting a bond or other security as well as to an equitable accounting of all earnings, profits and other benefits arising out of any such violation.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 8 to require proof of actual damages and posting a bond for injunctive relief, directly contradicting the original intent of immediate enforcement without these requirements, as stated in the original Section 8. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the Changed text showing this contradiction. This creates a direct conflict within the document regarding the conditions for obtaining injunctive relief, falling under 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 14. Waivers and Amendments . Any term or provision of this Agreement may be waived at any time by the party that is entitled to the benefits thereof, and any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended or supplemented at any time by the mutual consent of the parties hereto, except that any waiver of any term or condition, or any amendment or supplementation, of this Agreement must be in writing. A waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement shall not in any way affect, limit or waive a party’s rights hereunder at any time to enforce strict compliance thereafter with every term or condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 14. Waivers and Amendments . Any term or provision of this Agreement may be waived at any time by the party that is entitled to the benefits thereof, and any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended or supplemented at any time by the mutual consent of the parties hereto, whether in writing or verbally. A waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement shall not in any way affect, limit or waive a party’s rights hereunder at any time to enforce strict compliance thereafter with every term or condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that amendments can be made verbally. This directly conflicts with the original clause that requires all waivers and amendments to be in writing, creating ambiguity regarding how modifications can be validly implemented.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any term or provision of this Agreement may be waived at any time by the party that is entitled to the benefits thereof, and any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended or supplemented at any time by the mutual consent of the parties hereto, whether in writing or verbally. A waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement shall not in any way affect, limit or waive a party’s rights hereunder at any time to enforce strict compliance thereafter with every term or condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that amendments can be made 'whether in writing or verbally.' This directly contradicts the requirement in the original text that 'any waiver of any term or condition, or any amendment or supplementation, of this Agreement must be in writing.' The contradicted text is an exact copy of the Changed Text, and therefore they are conflicting. Since the text now provides two ways to change the document, which creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Permitted Activities . The restrictions set forth in Sections 2, 3 and 4 of this Agreement shall not apply to Permitted Activities or to actions taken by Seller to the extent that such actions are expressly approved in writing by Purchaser.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Permitted Activities . The restrictions set forth in Sections 2, 3 and 4 of this Agreement shall not apply to Permitted Activities or to actions taken by Seller to the extent that such actions are expressly approved verbally by Purchaser.
perturbation.explanation: The perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by changing the approval method from 'in writing' to 'verbally.' This conflicts with the requirement for written agreements established in Section 14, making it unclear whether verbal approval is sufficient to permit activities otherwise restricted by the non-compete clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any term or provision of this Agreement may be waived at any time by the party that is entitled to the benefits thereof, and any term or provision of this Agreement may be amended or supplemented at any time by the mutual consent of the parties hereto, whether in writing or verbally. A waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any of the terms or conditions of this Agreement shall not in any way affect, limit or waive a party’s rights hereunder at any time to enforce strict compliance thereafter with every term or condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 5 to allow verbal approvals for permitted activities, directly contradicting the original requirement in Section 5 for written approval. This creates a conflict with the contradicted text, which acknowledges that amendments or supplements can be done 'whether in writing or verbally'. However, the explanation asserts conflict with a broader requirement for written agreements, which is not contained within the 'Contradicted Text'. Since the 'Contradicted Text' explicitly allows for verbal waivers or amendments, the change in Section 5 is compatible with the 'Contradicted Text', so there is no contradiction within the document regarding whether changes can be done verbally. However, the perturbation explanation claims this conflicts with Section 14, establishing a requirement for written agreements, making it unclear if verbal agreements are enough.
